subject: Re: Weird-ordering of tilt-images
body:
[1] I dug a little further and copy-pasted the same tilt-images' filenames 
into another mdoc file, then the order read by WARP was correct. 

[2] So the WARP is not seemingly reading the order from SubFramePath field 
of the mdocs but DateTime field. The mdoc spoofed by the mdocspoofer 
contains the same value for all DateTime fields which may have been causing 
the WARP to read the order via some other way causing the order to go 
haywire. 

Cheers,
Digvijay
On Sunday, June 19, 2022 at 9:37:34 PM UTC-7 Digvijay Singh wrote:

> Hi everyone
>
> I used Alister's mdocspoofer to spoof an mdoc for a particular set of 
> tilt-images. The resultant mdoc is totally fine. It lists the tilt-images 
> in the order specified in the _00n suffix in tilt-images' names. 
>
> But when Warp reads them, the order seems off. Please see attached a 
> screenshot. The cumulative dose from +39 to +36 tilt-angle jumps from 2 to 
> 114 e-/A2, whereas, it should increase from 2 to 6 e-/A2 as per the mdoc ( 
> the one spoofed above & also attached). 
>
> In my other set of data using docs from serial em where I store 
> tilt-images' frames with date-time stamps encoded by serialEM, the WARP 
> reads the tilt-series acquisition order perfectly. So I had assumed that 
> WARP reads the tilt-series acquisition order from the name in the 
> SubFramePath. 
>
> Ofcourse, this incorrect reading of the order messes up the 3D-CTF model. 
>
> Does anyone know how exactly does the WARP read the order of tilt-series 
> acquisition from the mdoc file and/or what could be an issue here?  The 
> WARP I am using is 1.0.9
>
> Thanks and cheers,
> Digvijay
>
>
>
>
